steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence reads: "The sand at Komodo Island Beach in Indonesia is a more unusual [blank] deposits of crushed coral and other organic matter lend the sand a unique pink hue." Two independent clauses must be joined. "Though" here is a transitional adverb (meaning "however") placed between them, not a subordinating conjunction starting the second clause. The correct pattern is: IC, though; IC.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'shade, though;': The comma after "shade" sets off the transitional adverb "though," and the semicolon correctly joins the second independent clause. This follows the IC, [adverb]; IC pattern. ✓
  • Choice B — 'shade, though,': Two commas with a transitional adverb between independent clauses creates a comma splice — the second clause is not properly joined. ✗
  • Choice C — 'shade; though': The semicolon comes before "though," making "though deposits..." look like a subordinate clause starting the second segment, which is grammatically incorrect here. ✗
  • Choice D — 'shade, though': One comma only — this creates a comma splice between the two independent clauses. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
